steps.title: Set up proportion using arc length formula
steps.content:
The arc length $L$ relates to the circumference $C$ and central angle $\theta$ (in degrees) by $$L = \frac{\theta}{360} \cdot C.$$
Substitute the given values $L = 3$ inches and $\theta = 36^\circ$: $$3 = \frac{36}{360} \cdot C.$$
Simplify the fraction: $$\frac{36}{360} = \frac{1}{10}.$$

steps.title: Solve for circumference
steps.content:
From the simplified equation $$3 = \frac{1}{10} C,$$ multiply both sides by 10 to isolate $C$: $$C = 3 \times 10 = 30$$ inches.
That matches choice D — D is correct.
